title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot visits Valerie Hobhouse at 26 Walnut Street on a Sunday to discuss the investigation. Jerome leads Poirot to Valerie’s spacious, tastefully decorated room, which contains furniture Poirot notes is not original to the residence. Valerie, a buyer and stakeholder for a beauty salon called Sedna Goddess who travels frequently to Europe, appears tired and anxious. She mentions having been questioned by Inspector Sharp the previous day.

During their conversation, Poirot reveals that he has borrowed the engagement ring stolen from Patricia Rene to have it examined by a jeweler. He discloses a significant discovery: the large gemstone in the ring is not a diamond, but a white zircon. While Valerie suggests that Patricia may have replaced a lost diamond with a cheaper stone due to financial reasons, Poirot proposes a different theory. He suggests that the diamond was deliberately removed and replaced with a zircon before the ring was returned.

Poirot accuses Valerie of stealing the diamond. He explains his reasoning by analyzing how the ring was "discovered" in her soup bowl. He notes that the soup was brought to the table covered with a lid, meaning only the person serving the food or the person eating it could have placed the ring inside. Poirot concludes that Jerome, the server, did not do it, and instead accuses Valerie of staging the discovery of the ring in her own bowl for her own amusement and sense of humor, thereby exposing her involvement.
